shortDescription.text: PHP toolchain that analyzes your entire codebase in seconds, not minutes.

fullDescription.text: Mago is a toolchain for PHP that aims to provide a set of tools to help developers write better code

shortDescription.text: Detects the use of the `empty()` construct.

fullDescription.text:
Detects the use of the `empty()` construct.

The `empty()` language construct can lead to ambiguous and potentially buggy code due to
loose and counterintuitive definition of emptiness. It fails to clearly convey
developer's intent or expectation, making it preferable to use explicit checks.

shortDescription.text: Detects the use of the `isset()` construct.

fullDescription.text:
Detects the use of the `isset()` construct.

The `isset()` language construct checks whether a variable is set and is not null.
However, it can lead to ambiguous code because it conflates two distinct checks:
variable existence and null comparison. Using explicit null checks or the null
coalescing operator (`??`) is often clearer and more maintainable.

shortDescription.text: Detects the use of the `global` keyword and the `$GLOBALS` variable.

fullDescription.text:
Detects the use of the `global` keyword and the `$GLOBALS` variable.

The `global` keyword introduces global state into your function, making it harder to reason about and test.

shortDescription.text: Suggests using early continue pattern when a loop body contains only a single if statement.

fullDescription.text:
Suggests using early continue pattern when a loop body contains only a single if statement.

This improves code readability by reducing nesting and making the control flow more explicit.

shortDescription.text: Detects classes, traits, interfaces, functions, and closures with high kan defect.

fullDescription.text:
Detects classes, traits, interfaces, functions, and closures with high kan defect.

The "Kan Defect" metric is a heuristic for estimating defect proneness in a class or similar structure.
It counts control-flow statements (`while`, `do`, `foreach`, `if`, and `switch`) and sums them using a
formula loosely based on the work of Stephen H. Kan.

References:
  - https://github.com/phpmetrics/PhpMetrics/blob/c43217cd7783bbd54d0b8c1dd43f697bc36ef79d/src/Hal/Metric/Class_/Complexity/KanDefectVisitor.php
  - https://phpmetrics.org/

shortDescription.text: Detects functions, closures, and methods with too many parameters.

fullDescription.text:
Detects functions, closures, and methods with too many parameters.

If the number of parameters exceeds a configurable threshold, an issue is reported.

shortDescription.text: Checks the cyclomatic complexity of classes, traits, enums, interfaces, functions, and closures.

fullDescription.text:
Checks the cyclomatic complexity of classes, traits, enums, interfaces, functions, and closures.

Cyclomatic complexity is a measure of the number of linearly independent paths through a program's source code.

shortDescription.text: Enforces the use of curly braces around variables within string interpolation.

fullDescription.text:
Enforces the use of curly braces around variables within string interpolation.

Using curly braces (`{$variable}`) within interpolated strings ensures clarity and avoids potential ambiguity,
especially when variables are followed by alphanumeric characters. This rule promotes consistent and predictable code.

shortDescription.text: Detects class-like structures with too many methods.

fullDescription.text:
Detects class-like structures with too many methods.

This rule checks the number of methods in classes, traits, enums, and interfaces.
If the number of methods exceeds a configurable threshold, an issue is reported.

shortDescription.text: Detects class-like structures with too many properties.

fullDescription.text:
Detects class-like structures with too many properties.

This rule checks the number of properties in classes, traits, and interfaces.
If the number of properties exceeds a configurable threshold, an issue is reported.

shortDescription.text: Flags `if` statements that include an `else` or `elseif` branch.

fullDescription.text:
Flags `if` statements that include an `else` or `elseif` branch.

Using `else` or `elseif` can lead to deeply nested code and complex control flow.
This can often be simplified by using early returns (guard clauses), which makes
the code easier to read and maintain by reducing its cyclomatic complexity.

shortDescription.text: Computes Halstead metrics (volume, difficulty, effort) and reports if they exceed configured thresholds.

fullDescription.text:
Computes Halstead metrics (volume, difficulty, effort) and reports if they exceed configured thresholds.

Halstead metrics are calculated by counting operators and operands in the analyzed code.
For more info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halstead_complexity_measures

shortDescription.text: Detects the use of the error control operator `@`.

fullDescription.text:
Detects the use of the error control operator `@`.

The error control operator suppresses errors and makes debugging more difficult.

shortDescription.text: Flags any instances of multiple assignments in a single statement. This can lead to

fullDescription.text:
Flags any instances of multiple assignments in a single statement. This can lead to
confusion and unexpected behavior, and is generally considered poor practice.

shortDescription.text: Detects functions relying on loose comparison unless the `$strict` parameter is specified.

fullDescription.text:
Detects functions relying on loose comparison unless the `$strict` parameter is specified.
The use of loose comparison for these functions may lead to hard-to-debug, unexpected behaviors.

shortDescription.text: Flags function-like parameters that use a boolean type.

fullDescription.text:
Flags function-like parameters that use a boolean type.

Boolean flag parameters can indicate a violation of the Single Responsibility Principle (SRP).
Refactor by extracting the flag logic into its own class or method.

shortDescription.text: Detects `use` statements that import items that are never used or are redundant

fullDescription.text:
Detects `use` statements that import items that are never used or are redundant
because they import from the same namespace.

shortDescription.text: Detects equality and inequality comparisons that should use identity comparison operators.

fullDescription.text: Detects equality and inequality comparisons that should use identity comparison operators.

shortDescription.text: Detects assignments in conditions which can lead to unexpected behavior and make the code harder

fullDescription.text:
Detects assignments in conditions which can lead to unexpected behavior and make the code harder
to read and understand.

shortDescription.text: Mago SARIF Converter

description.text: The root directory for all project files
The root directory for this project files

message.text:
Use of the `empty` construct.
>Ambiguous check due to `empty()` loose semantic

`empty()` exhibits unexpected behavior on specific value.
It is unclear what condition is being treated with `empty()`.

Help: Use strict comparison or specific predicate function to clearly convey your intent.

Use of the `isset` construct.
>Ambiguous check due to `isset()` conflating existence and null comparison

`isset()` returns false for both unset variables and null values.
It is unclear whether you're checking for variable existence or non-null value.

Help: Use explicit null comparison (`!== null`) or the null coalescing operator (`??`).

Use of the `empty` construct.
>Ambiguous check due to `empty()` loose semantic

`empty()` exhibits unexpected behavior on specific value.
It is unclear what condition is being treated with `empty()`.

Help: Use strict comparison or specific predicate function to clearly convey your intent.

Unsafe use of `$GLOBALS` variable.
>The `$GLOBALS` variable is used here

Accessing the `$GLOBALS` array directly can lead to similar issues as using the `global` keyword.
It can make your code harder to understand, test, and maintain due to the implicit global state.
Consider using dependency injection or other techniques to manage state and avoid relying on global variables.

Help: Refactor your code to avoid using the `$GLOBALS` variable directly.

Consider using early continue pattern to reduce nesting.
>This if statement wraps the entire loop body
>The loop can benefit from early continue to improve readability

This pattern improves readability by reducing nesting levels.

Help: Invert the condition and use `continue` to exit early, then place the main logic outside the if block.

Use of the `empty` construct.
>Ambiguous check due to `empty()` loose semantic

`empty()` exhibits unexpected behavior on specific value.
It is unclear what condition is being treated with `empty()`.

Help: Use strict comparison or specific predicate function to clearly convey your intent.

Kan defect is a heuristic used by phpmetrics to estimate defect-proneness based on control-flow statements.

Having a large number of parameters can make functions harder to understand and maintain.

Help: Try reducing the number of parameters, or consider passing an object or a shape instead.

Class has high complexity.
>Class has a cyclomatic complexity of 18, which exceeds the threshold of 15.

Unbraced variable in string interpolation.
>String interpolation contains unbraced variables
>Variable should be enclosed in curly braces

Using curly braces around variables in interpolated strings improves readability and prevents potential parsing issues.

Help: Wrap the variable in curly braces, e.g., `{$variable}`.

Use identity comparison `===` instead of equality comparison `==`.
>Equality operator is used here

Identity comparison `===` checks for both value and type equality, while equality comparison `==` performs type coercion, which can lead to unexpected results.

Help: Use `===` to ensure both value and type are equal.

Avoid assignments in conditions.
>This is an assignment
>This is the condition

Assigning a value within a condition can lead to unexpected behavior and make the code harder to read and understand.
It's easy to confuse assignment (`=`) with comparison (`==`) in this context. Ensure you're using the correct operator.
